sections.sustainability: M&S reaffirmed its 'Trusted Value' strategy with hard sustainability targets attached: all cotton in clothing now comes from more responsible sources, and it aims to source 100% of polyester from verified recycled materials by end of 2026, up from 91% currently. No new Plan A, GOTS, OEKO-TEX or RWS announcements surfaced this week.
sections.strategy: M&S confirmed its first-ever London Fashion Week runway slot for 18 September — a 'see now, buy now' womenswear and menswear collection timed to the 100th anniversary of M&S fashion, shown alongside Burberry and Mulberry. Store transformation continues: its biggest-ever standalone Foodhall (22,000 sq ft, Godalming, Surrey) opened 16 July, and the refurbished Pantheon Oxford Street flagship is now the testing ground for future store design.
sections.pricing: M&S cut prices on over 65 shopping-list essentials as part of a £30m investment to help families 'eat well for less', and expanded the Remarksable Value range to 175 products with 29 new everyday staples added this week, alongside further additions to Family Value Lines.
sections.launches: Fashion launch momentum is high: the 'Dress To Thrill' collection dropped as the second instalment of the 'Summer of Love' campaign, following the Silverstone pit-lane fashion show ahead of the F1 British Grand Prix (50 models, building on an Ibiza show seen by 26m). M&S also launched an 'Autograph Performance' menswear line and extended Amazon availability into the Netherlands.
sections.gossip: City sentiment remains strongly positive: shares around 378p, up ~16% YTD (more than double the FTSE 100), with 16 buy ratings, zero sells, and an average 12-month price target of 431p (high 500p). Executives added shares via the Dividend Reinvestment Plan on 10 July at ~375p. Analysts continue to flag that fashion-relevance progress must be sustained across multiple seasons.
soWhat: The 'see now, buy now' London Fashion Week format (18 Sept) means M&S will demand faster speed-to-market from its supply base — suppliers who can compress lead times for runway-to-store drops will be favoured.
The 100% verified recycled polyester target by end-2026 (currently 91%) makes verified-recycled/GRS certification effectively mandatory for polyester-based suppliers wanting M&S business this year.

Heavy investment in fashion credibility (LFW debut, Silverstone, Autograph Performance) plus unanimous City buy ratings points to growing Clothing & Home volumes — a pitch window for premium and performance fabric capability, especially around Autograph.
No supplier-map or sourcing-mix changes surfaced — the India/Sri Lanka/Bangladesh/Turkey/China balance appears stable, with no signal of imminent reallocation.
